characters.name: Hero
characters.description: TheHeroesare those who possess extraordinary powers beyond general human evolution. Most of these people have attempted to use their powers for good, but some have wielded them towards more negative goals, using them to steal, overpower, or murder others. The primary theory behind their creation is that an enzyme or chemical that spreads across genetic lines grants them their powers. Among some, this chemical may have occurred as a result ofevolution; however, it is revealed near the end of Volume Three that indeed, some have received their abilities due to human intervention usingThe Formula. Dr.Mohinder Sureshhas come to surmise that the powers are activated through adrenal glands, in the exception ofRapid Cellular RegenerationandEmpathic Mimicry, some have found ways to master their abilities and can activate them at will.

characters.name: Abby Collins
characters.description: Abby CollinsBiographical InformationFull nameAbigail CollinsGenderFemaleStatusActiveOccupationsHomeland Security AgentResidenceWashington D.CProductionPortrayed ByMoira KellyFirst episodeBuilding 26 Abby was first introduced in the Season 3 episode,Building 26. She attended a meeting ofNathan PetrelliandThe Hunters', at which she told Nathan that she was going to shut his operation down due to the fact that he had kept prisoners without Miranda rights. Abby was later taken to see a captive,Tracy Straussupon which she identified the blonde as such and inquired as to why she was being kept under heat lamps. Still not buying into the whole idea of humans having powers, Abby left with a promise to return with the proper documentation to shut the project down. Later, Abby did indeed return with the papers stating that Nathan's project was shut down, but after a freak accident involving Tracy escaping and killing someone with her icy powers, Abby, a witness to the incident, agreed that the project was valid and needed to stay open. characters.name: Ando Masahashi (Villains future)
characters.description: Ando MasahashiBiographical InformationNickname(s)Ando kunGenderMalePowersPower AugmentationOccupationsAgent ofPinehearst CompanyResidenceTokyo, JapanFamily InformationRelationshipsHiro Nakamura(former Friend)Ando Masahashiis a agent atPinehearst Company. He is a EVO with the ability ofPower amplifying. In "The Second Coming"Hiro Nakamuratravels to the future, he looks up at a balcony and sees future Ando along with himself, they're arguing, Ando demands that Hiro hands over the other half of the formula but he denies Ando and pulls out his sword and goes in to attack but Ando blasts Hiro with a red energy bolt thus killing him and then he takes his half of the formula and leaves just before Tokyo is destroyed by Tsunami. In "The Second Coming"Hiro Nakamuratravels to the future, he looks up at a balcony and sees future Ando along with himself, they're arguing, Ando demands that Hiro hands over the other half of the formula but he denies Ando and pulls out his sword and goes in to attack but Ando blasts Hiro with a red energy bolt thus killing him and then he takes his half of the formula and leaves just before Tokyo is destroyed by Tsunami.

characters.name: Charlie Andrews
characters.description: Charlie AndrewsBiographical InformationNickname(s)WkipediaGoogleMary JaneLittle Susie FlapjacksAdorable-CharlieSweet, Loving, Perfect, CharlieGenderFemaleAge85+StatusDeceasedPowersEnhanced MemoryOccupationsWaitress (formerly)Factory worker (formerly)ResidenceMidland, Texas(formerly)Milwaukee, WisconsinFamily InformationFamilyUnnamed husbandUnnamed Parents (deceased)Sally(granddaughter)Four unnamed childrenSix unnamed grandchildrenRelationshipsHiro Nakamura(former lover)ProductionPortrayed ByJayma Mays(adult)K Callan(elderly)First episodeSeven Minutes to MidnightLast episodeBrave New WorldCharlene "Charlie" Andrews, portrayed byJayma Mays, is a waitress at the Burnt Toast Diner in Midland, Texas, whereHiro NakamuraandAndo Masahashistop to eat on their road trip to New York. After she reveals to Hiro that she had recently developed the power to quickly memorize and recall any information with great accuracy in a seemingly unlimited volume,Sylarmurders her in the diner's storeroom, removing her brain in the same manner as his other victims. Hiro tries to prevent her murder by going back in time one day to warn her, but he accidentally goes back six months, to April 24 (Charlie's birthday). He convinces Charlie that both he and she have special powers by stopping time to make one thousand origami cranes. After taking a job at the diner and dating Charlie, Hiro buys two tickets to take her to Tokyo. Charlie ponders her feelings and tells Hiro that while she does not usually let people get close to her due to a blood clot in her brain that is killing her, he makes her feel happy and she loves him. However, when Hiro and Charlie are about to kiss, he inadvertently teleports back to present day Japan and is unable to return. WhileHirois in hospital he manages to teleport back in time to before Charlie died. She is very pleased to see Hiro and gives him a big hug. After this Hiro saves Charlie's life by removingSylar. Sylar gets annoyed and threatens Hiro. Charlie is suffering with her brain clot so Hiro makes a deal with Sylar, telling him how he will die so he will save Charlie's life and not kill either of them. Sylar does so, and Charlie is healed. Charlie and Hiro get ready to go on a trip to Japan, however one ofSamuel'smen Arnold takes Charlie away so Hiro will do something for him. After she reveals to Hiro that she had recently developed the power to quickly memorize and recall any information with great accuracy in a seemingly unlimited volume,Sylarmurders her in the diner's storeroom, removing her brain in the same manner as his other victims. Hiro tries to prevent her murder by going back in time one day to warn her, but he accidentally goes back six months, to April 24 (Charlie's birthday). He convinces Charlie that both he and she have special powers by stopping time to make one thousand origami cranes. After taking a job at the diner and dating Charlie, Hiro buys two tickets to take her to Tokyo. Charlie ponders her feelings and tells Hiro

characters.name: Angela Petrelli (Infection future)
characters.description: Angela PetrelliSort NamePetrelli, Angela (Infection future)Portrayed byCristine RoseFirst appearanceOut of TimeIn-story statsGenderFemaleOccupationsFounding memberofthe CompanySignificant othersformerlyArthur PetrelliChildrenNathan Petrelli(deceased),Peter PetrelliGrandchildrenClaire Bennet,Simon Petrelli,Monty Petrelli Angela Petrelliis the mother ofNathanandPeter Petrelliin2008, living in a virus-strickenNew York. WhenPeteraccidentallytime travelsinto theInfection futurewithno memory, Angela is brought to help him try to remember. Upon seeing her son, Angela hugs him but Peter pushes her away due to his lack of recognition. Angela describes who Peter is to him and describes Peter's powers, telling him that heabsorbs the powers of those around himand is the most powerful of them all. Angela tells Peter that he had a brother namedNathanwho died in the first outbreak, but Peter can change it. Peter demands to know how since the last thing he knew he was in Montreal in 2007 and is now there with her. Angela explains that Peter met a man namedHiro Nakamurawho cantravel in timeand as a result so can Peter. Angela tells Peter that he can use his power to change history, but a frustrated Peter tells Angela that he can't control what's happening to him. Angela tells Peter that he must because the virus will kill everyone otherwise. As Angela insists that Peter has to remember, Peter inadvertently beginsreading her mindand sees several events of his life from her perspective and finally realizes Angela is his mother. The two embrace and Angela leads Peter out of the building, telling him they are going home like the people in the deportation line. Peter leaves her and rushes over to Caitlin when he spots her and attempts to return himself and Caitlin to the right time. Peter gets separated from Caitlin and returns to his own time inadvertently, leaving Angela and Caitlin behind.

characters.name: Annapura
characters.description: AnnapuraBiographical InformationNickname(s)AnnaGenderFemaleStatusActiveOccupationsBakery OwnerResidenceNew Delhi, IndiaProductionPortrayed ByAmrapali AmbegaokarFirst episodeBuilding 26 Annapura was the woman depicted in Parkman's painting ofHiro Nakamurain New Delhi, India. She is a bakery owner who is afraid to marry her thuggish fiancee,Deepak. However, she meetsAndo Masahashiand thinks that he is a sign from God that she prayed for to tell her not to marryDeepak. After a series of events that ends up with Ando a prisoner of Deepak, Hiro takes things upon himself and stops Annapura's wedding and punches Deepak in the face, requesting that the marriage not happen and that Ando is returned. His requests are met and Annapura is happy. Later and back at her bakery, she realizes that Ando's name seemed familiar when he told it to her. He and Hiro had received a fax from the mysteriousRebelthat had been textingClaire Bennet.

characters.name: Anne Clark
characters.description: Anne ClarkBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveOccupationsNurseFamily InformationFamilyHiro Nakamura(husband)Tommy Clark(adoptive son)RelationshipsCaspar Abraham(ally)ProductionPortrayed ByKrista BridgesFirst episodeBrave New World (Reborn)Last episodeProject RebornAnne Clarkportrayed byKrista Bridgesis a recurring character on the NBC science fiction drama seriesHeroes Reborn. She isTommy Clark's adoptive mother, who first appeared in "Brave New World (Reborn)". It was later shown that she is not actually Tommy's mother.Hiro NakamurawasTommy Clark's original guardian but then she married him and they became a family, Hiro sacrificed himself so they could get away and Anne has been raising Tommy by herself ever since. In "Brave New World (Reborn)", Anne is driving in her car withTommyto the Canadian border, they noticed people are being screened to test if they are a EVO, so she drives off in the opposite direction. In "Under the Mask", Anne comes inTommy'sroom and notices that he's doing push up and has on his favorite shirt, she realizes that he's clearly trying to impress agirl. She asks him who is the girl but Tommy doesn't answer, then she asks him where is he going. Tommy says that he's going toBrad'shouse to play video games but she doesn't believe him and tells him to stay home. Tommy disagrees with this decision, he tells her that he'll be fine, especially since he hassomeoneprotecting him. She wants to know who it is but Tommy doesn't know either and he then leaves the house anyways.Anne shows up outside of Brad's house and points a gun at Caspar's face, she wants to know if he's the protector, she seems to recognize him and then she tells him to stay away and then she gets Tommy and makes him get in the car, they ride off but they don't get far, they are rammed from the side by a truck.

characters.name: Arnold
characters.description: This article or section is astub.You can help Heroes Wiki byexpanding it. ArnoldBiographical InformationStatusDeceasedPowersSpace-Time ManipulationOccupationsSullivan Bros. Carnivalemployee,former chefFamily InformationFamilyMelvin Crum (deceased)ProductionPortrayed ByJack WallaceFirst episode1961Arnoldwas a member of theSullivan Bros. Carnivaland a close friend toJoseph Sullivan. Aftertraveling back in timeto the year of 1961, Arnold was trapped in the past and forced to live out his life with no memory of himself or the carnival. After more than thirty years, Arnold was reunited with Joseph,Lydia, andSamuel, and was returned to the carnival. Dying from a brain tumor, Arnold was killed by Samuel. He was anAdvanced Humanwith the ability tomanipulate the space-time continuum. Arnold possessed the ability to travel forward and backwards through time. He was able to transport at least one other individual with him at any given time as well as transport just himself. Last year, Arnold began to have headaches after accessing his ability and as his life progressed he developed a brain tumor that caused him pain every time he would use his power.

He was anevolved humanwith the ability tosteal other evolved humans' abilities through physical contact. He served as the primary antagonist ofVolume Three. characters.name: Arthur Petrelli

characters.name: Barbara
characters.description: BarbaraBiographical InformationBirthAugust 24, 1982 at 12:56Age28 "2010StatusAlivePowersEnhanced Speed(suspected)Family InformationFamilyJonas Zimmerman(guardian)Niki Sanders†Tracy Strauss(sister)Micah Sanders(nephew)ProductionPortrayed ByAli LarterFirst episodeFrom the Files of Primatech, Part 7 Barbarais one in the set of triplets (along withNiki SandersandTracy Strauss) who was synthetically given abilities by Dr. Zimmerman. After being separated by her sisters, Barbara appears to have been raised by Dr. Zimmerman as his own daughter. Zimmerman mentioned super strength and super speed upon meeting Tracy Strauss. Considering that Niki Sanders had the ability of super strength, one can assume that Barbara has the ability of super speed. Dr. Zimmerman, the man who delivered Niki and Tracy, initially confuses Tracy for Barbara when she visits his home in Reseda.

characters.name: Lyle Bennet
characters.description: This article or section is astub.You can help Heroes Wiki byexpanding it. Lyle BennetBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveOccupationsStudentFamily InformationFamilyNoah BennetSandra BennetClaire Bennet(adoptive sister)ProductionPortrayed ByRandall BentleyFirst episodeGenesisLyle BennetisClaire Bennet's adoptive brother andNoah BennetandSandra Bennet's biological son. Lyle is the younger adoptive brother of Claire Bennet, He made his first appearance inGenesis. He appeares to be a pre- teen boy, and enjoys picking on his sister. He discovers that his adoptive sister Claire has powers However, he forgets this when his memory wiped. He is still living during the events ofFour Months Later. characters.name: Noah Bennet
characters.description: Noah BennetBiographical InformationNickname(s)HRGGlassesThe Man in The Horn-Rimmed GlassesThe Man With The PlanMr. Bag and TagWolfgangAbu GhraibThe Boogey ManNoah ButlerTed BarnesGenderMaleAge53StatusDeceasedOccupationsCar salesman (formerly)Employee ofCopy Kingdom(formerly)Manager ofPrimatech Paper(formerly)Agent ofHomeland Security(formerly)Chairman ofThe Company(formerly)Field Manager ofThe Company(formerly)ResidenceAustin, TexasOdessa, TexasNew York City, New YorkCosta Verde, CaliforniaLos Angeles, CaliforniaWashington, D.C.Family InformationFamilyUnnamed FatherMalina Bennet (mother)Kate Bennet(1st ex-wife)Sandra Bennet(2nd ex-wife)Lyle Bennet(son)Claire Bennet(adoptive daughter)Unborn childTommy Clark(grandson)Malina(granddaughter)RelationshipsJulia(fiance)Lauren Gilmore(lover)ProductionPortrayed ByJack ColemanFirst episodeGenesisLast episodeProject RebornNoah Bennetwas a major character and anti-hero from theNBCdramaHeroesplayed byJack Coleman. In theHeroes Rebornseries he has become the hero. At first glance, Noah Bennet appears to be an ordinary businessman who works at thePrimatech Paper Companyand lives in Odessa, Texas, with his wife,Sandra, and two children,ClaireandLyle. However, he and his associates have actually traveled the world for a number of years investigating superhuman phenomena and tracking down and experimenting on people with these abilities. Bennet lives in a world of "morally gray" and is considered a Company Man, doing anything the company asks of him. However, his true allegiance is to his family. Noah Bennet first appears in "Genesis", the first episode. He is found in the apartment ofChandra Sureshin Madras, India. Chandra's sonMohinderenters the apartment, but leaves quickly after realizing Bennet is there as well. Bennet seems to notice Mohinder and later follows him to New York. There, Bennet is one of Mohinder's afternoon passengers during his job as a taxicab driver. Bennet begins pleasantly referencing his travels and stating that he'll be happy to see his family again. The conversation turns as he mentions personal facts about Mohinder, ultimately resulting in Mohinder fleeing his own cab. Finally, Bennet comes home to Odessa and greets his family. The next morning in "Don't Look Back", Bennet's daughter Claire states her desire to meet her biological parents. Bennet is reluctant, but doesn't outright say "no". After Claire gets home from school, Bennet says he'll start contacting the adoption agency and look for her biological parents. After a happy Claire leaves the room, Bennet is seen watching a videotape of Claire demonstrating her healing abilities. At first glance, Noah Bennet appears to be an ordinary businessman who works at thePrimatech Paper Companyand lives in Odessa, Texas, with his wife,Sandra, and two children,ClaireandLyle. However, he and his associates have actually traveled the world for a number of years investigating superhuman phenomena and tracking down and experimenting on people wit

characters.name: Sandra Bennet
characters.description: This article or section is in need of a cleanup.It may be poorly written and/or have grammatical errors. You can help Heroes Wiki bycleaning it up. Sandra BennetBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAlivePowersNoneOccupationsHousewifeResidenceCosta Verde, CaliforniaFamily InformationFamilyLyle Bennet(son)Claire Bennet(adoptive daughter)ProductionPortrayed ByAshley CrowFirst episodeGenesisSandra BennetisClaire Bennet's adoptive mother. She was the wife ofNoah Bennetbut she ended their relationship after a barrage of lies. She is also the mother ofLyle Bennetand guardian ofMr. Muggles. Sandra currently lives inCosta Verde, CAwith her son while Claire is away attending college. For an episode-by-episode summary, seeSandra Bennet: Season One History.[1][2]Sandra spends quality time withher daughter.Mrs. Bennet is protective ofClaire: She tries to persuadeher husbandnot to give in to her daughter's request to see her birth parents; when Claire needsstitches for her cut hand, it is Sandra who accompanies her to thehospital. Also, when Claire ends up in acar accident, Mrs. Bennet is first to arrive at the emergency room.

characters.name: Gretchen Berg
characters.description: Gretchen BergProductionPortrayed ByMadeline ZimaFirst episodeOrientation Gretchen Bergis the roommate ofClairein college, after Annie was pushed out of a window and killed by Becky. Not much is known about Gretchen, except the fact that she has a crush on Claire Bennet. Becky, Samuel's "niece" killed Claire's first roommate Annie, by pushing her out a window, and now tried to kill Gretchen twice while on their "scavenger hunt", but both times Claire saves her.Noah Bennetbrings Gretchen to his apartment to cheer up Claire, then Claire takes the compass and goes with Gretchen to the Sullivan Bros. Carnival. Not much is known about Gretchen, except the fact that she has a crush on Claire Bennet. Carnival.

characters.name: Big guy
characters.description: Big guyBiographical InformationGenderMaleOccupationsThugProductionPortrayed ByMilos MilicevicFirst episodeGenesisBig guyworked forMr. Linderman, until he went with his partner,Weasel, to extort money fromNiki Sanders. However,Jessicatook over Niki and killed both of them. Niki discovered the body of the big guy. Big guy and Weasel knocked on Niki andMicah'shome door, intending to collect money that Niki owesMr. Linderman. When they received no answer, the big guy broke down the door. Later on, as Niki found that her home had been trashed, the big guy recorded Niki as the weasel forced her to stripe. After being punched in the face by the weasel, Niki was horrified to find the body of the big guyripped apartby her alter ego,Jessica.[1] Big guy and Weasel knocked on Niki andMicah'shome door, intending to collect money that Niki owesMr. Linderman. When they received no answer, the big guy broke down the door. Later on, as Niki found that her home had been trashed, the big guy recorded Niki as the weasel forced her to stripe. After being punched in the face by the weasel, Niki was horrified to find the body of the big guyripped apartby her alter ego,Jessica.[1] After a while, Niki found the body of the big guy brutally stuffed in the trunk of a Cadillac.[2]Niki buried the bodies in the Nevada Desert.[3]

characters.name: Eleanor Zoe Bishop
characters.description: This article or section is in need of a cleanup.It may be poorly written and/or have grammatical errors. You can help Heroes Wiki bycleaning it up. Eleanor Zoe BishopBiographical InformationNickname(s)ElleGenderFemaleAge24(at time of death)StatusDeceasedPowersElectricity ManipulationOccupationsThe Companyagent - formerlyPinehearstagentFamily InformationFamilyRobert Bishop- father †ProductionPortrayed ByKristen Bell Eleanor Zoe Bishop, also known asElle, was the daughter ofRobert Bishop. She was a sadistic sociopath who delighted in treating people like toys. From the age of seven, she was tortured by her father, who pushed her so hard that she broke. She had the power to generate electricity. She served as a recurring antagonist inVolume TwoandVolume Three. She was killed bySylar. Elle constantly exhibited childlike and sadistic behaviour. She treated people like toys and often got a kick out of watching them suffer. She enjoyed shocking numerous people with her power just for the fun of it. When it came to her past Elle was incredibly private. She enjoyed being in control of the situation and the people around her. So far she had only ever opened up to Peter about her life. According to her she set her grandmothers house on fire when she was six, caused a blackout in four counties in Ohio when she was eight and spent her ninth birthday in a glass room with an IV of lithium in her arm. She endured brutal testing from her father as a child which could account for her numerous psychological problems. After losing her father and job Elle became increasingly depressed and wallowed in all the terrible things she had done in the past which caused her power to rage out of her control. She was constantly seeking approval and affection from her pushy and often cruel father but never got it. When she learned of some of the things he had done to her in her childhood she became increasingly wary of him and showed great sadness when he was dead. However she still tried desperately to exact vengeance on Sylar for killing him.

characters.name: Robert "Bob" Bishop
characters.description: This article or section is under construction.It is in the middle ofan expansion or a major revamping.You are welcome to help in it's construction by editing it well. Robert BishopBiographical InformationFull nameRobert BishopNickname(s)BobBobbyGenderMaleStatusDeceasedPowersAlchemyFamily InformationFamilyElle Bishop(daughter)ProductionPortrayed ByStephen TobolowskyH. Michael Croner(in1961)First episodeFour Months Later...Last episode1961Robert "Bob" Bishopwas one of twelve founding members ofThe Company, and father toElle Bishop. He had the ability ofAlchemy. Bob was killed whenSylarhad gained entry to his private office at Primatech, enabling Sylar to take Bob's power. "She's right. We're freaks and they're going to make us normal."- Bob Bishop,1961

characters.name: Brad
characters.description: BradBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusActiveOccupationsHigh school studentFamily InformationRelationshipsEmily Duval(ex-girlfriend)ProductionPortrayed ByJake ManleyFirst episodeBrave New World (Reborn)Bradis a football player and student at Pinehearst High school. He isEmily Duval'sex-boyfriend. In "Brave New World (Reborn)" Brad is in the hallway at school with his girlfriendEmilyand he seesTommylooking at her, he shoves Tommy against the locker and hits him in the stomach. In "Odessa" He catches Tommy in a shed by the school with Emily at displaying his ability ofTeleportation. He confronts Tommy on being a evo and he tells him that he's going to the authorities, Tommy convinces him that he isn't a bad guy and Brad agrees to keep his secret as long as he does him a favor first. He brings Tommy to his house and tells him to make his stepdad disappear because he's useless, aggressive and abusive, he leaves the room and tells Tommy that his stepdad better be gone by the time he returns. Brad later finds Tommy and gives him a hug and he thanks him because he thinks Tommy is the one who got rid of his stepdad.

characters.name: Brad
characters.description: BradBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusActiveOccupationsHigh school studentFamily InformationRelationshipsEmily Duval(ex-girlfriend)ProductionPortrayed ByJake ManleyFirst episodeBrave New World (Reborn)Bradis a football player and student at Pinehearst High school. He isEmily Duval'sex-boyfriend. In "Brave New World (Reborn)" Brad is in the hallway at school with his girlfriendEmilyand he seesTommylooking at her, he shoves Tommy against the locker and hits him in the stomach. In "Odessa" He catches Tommy in a shed by the school with Emily at displaying his ability ofTeleportation. He confronts Tommy on being a evo and he tells him that he's going to the authorities, Tommy convinces him that he isn't a bad guy and Brad agrees to keep his secret as long as he does him a favor first. He brings Tommy to his house and tells him to make his stepdad disappear because he's useless, aggressive and abusive, he leaves the room and tells Tommy that his stepdad better be gone by the time he returns. Brad later finds Tommy and gives him a hug and he thanks him because he thinks Tommy is the one who got rid of his stepdad.

characters.name: Brody Mitchum
characters.description: Brody MitchumBiographical InformationAge16-18StatusActiveOccupationsStudentProductionPortrayed ByMatt LanterFirst episodeDon't Look Back Brody Mitchumis a civilian student atUnion Wells High Schooland a minor antagonist inVolume One. In the episodeOne Giant Leaphe attempts to rapeClaire Bennet, accidentally 'killing' her in the process. InCollisionBrody is revealed to be a serial rapist, a fact which causes Claire to purposefully crash his car, with him inside of it, in an attempt to stop him from raping again.  He was Quarterback on the school football team, the Wildcats. This was untilThe Haitiancleared his memory.

characters.name: Luke Campbell
characters.description: Luke CampbellBiographical InformationFull nameLucas CampbellNickname(s)Luke (by everyone)GenderMaleStatusUnknownPowersMicrowave Ray RadiationOccupationsHigh School StudentProductionPortrayed ByDaniel ByrdFirst episodeTrust and Blood Luke Campbell is an advanced human who recently got into trouble for getting into a fight at school. After Sylar was attacked by Homeland Security Agents in his father, Samson Gray's home he took one of his attackers hostage in the Campbell's home and used screwdrivers to nailed him to a chair. After Luke demonstrates his power for Sylar in an effort to keep Sylar from torturing his mother Luke kills the agent with his power. Luke leaves his old life behind and joins Sylar on his journey to find his biological parents. According to Sylar, Luke possesses many traits that he does; Luke's father is not present, he dislikes his mother, he feels lonely and wants to be special, and possesses a power that makes him "special".
